title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a side of naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of our homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a true Indian classic perfect for any meal.

description: Savor the rich and creamy delight of Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic renowned for its tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of smoky tanginess and subtle sweetness, making it an irresistible choice for a comforting and flavorful meal.

ingredients.notes: from soaked cashews blended with water

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 tablespoon chili powder, turmeric, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well and marinate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned and almost cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Pour in the tomato puree, remaining 1/2 tablespoon Kashmiri chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon garam masala. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the mixture and the gravy thickens, about 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish the Gravy
instructions.text: Add the cashew paste, sugar, and crushed kasoori methi to the gravy. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. If the gravy is too thick, add a splash of water to reach desired consistency.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken thoroughly. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Add Cream and Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh cream and mix well. Do not boil after adding cream. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan, roti, or rice.

tips: For a deeper flavor, marinate the chicken overnight. Grilling or charring the chicken slightly before adding to the gravy adds a smoky tandoori flavor.
Using ripe, red tomatoes for the puree is crucial for the authentic color and sweet-tangy flavor of the makhani gravy. A little tomato paste can enhance the color if needed.
Always add fresh cream at the very end and avoid boiling the gravy afterward to prevent curdling and maintain its smooth, rich texture.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani: A Quintessential Indian Lunch Recipe

When it comes to the pantheon of Indian cuisine, few dishes command as much global adoration and culinary curiosity as Butter Chicken, known in its native Hindi as Murgh Makhani. This iconic dish, with its rich, creamy texture and tantalizing aroma, has transcended borders to become a beloved staple in homes and restaurants worldwide. Perfect for a hearty lunch or a special dinner, Butter Chicken is a testament to the magic that happens when simple ingredients are combined with skill and passion. If you're looking to explore the vibrant world of Indian lunch recipes, mastering Murgh Makhani is an excellent place to start.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani translates literally to "chicken with butter" or "butter chicken." Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, India, in the 1950s. The story goes that leftover tandoori chicken pieces were simmered in a rich tomato and butter-based gravy to prevent them from drying out. The result was a revelation: a mild, subtly sweet, and incredibly flavorful curry that quickly captured the hearts of diners.

What defines authentic Butter Chicken is its signature silky-smooth gravy. This gravy is typically made from tomatoes, cashews, and a generous amount of butter and cream, balanced with aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, garam masala, and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). Unlike many other Indian curries, Murgh Makhani is known for its relatively mild spice profile, making it a crowd-pleaser even for those who are sensitive to heat. The chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices and then grilled or pan-fried, retains its succulence and absorbs the glorious flavors of the sauce.

## Ingredients for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of ingredients. Here’s a breakdown of what you’ll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast), cut into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Murgh Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (400g) can crushed tomatoes or 3-4 medium fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, optional)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon sugar
*   1/2 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (or more, to taste)
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish
*   Salt to taste

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe

Follow these steps to create a restaurant-quality Butter Chicken in your own kitchen:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken**: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken**: Heat a little oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer (cook in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding). Cook for 5-7 minutes on each side, until browned and cooked through. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base**: In the same pan (or a clean one), melt the butter with the oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Aromatic Infusion**: Add the ginger-garlic paste to the onions and cook for another minute until fragrant.
5.  **Tomato & Cashew Base**: Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or tomato puree), soaked cashews (if using), red chili powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides.
6.  **Blend the Sauce**: Carefully transfer the tomato-cashew mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Simmer and Finish**: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low-medium heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces, sugar, and kasoori methi. Stir well and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce.
8.  **The Grand Finale**: Stir in the heavy cream and a final knob of butter. Heat gently for a few minutes, but do not boil after adding the cream, as it can curdle. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if needed.
9.  **Garnish and Serve**: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Your homemade Butter Chicken is now ready to be enjoyed!

## Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that perfect, authentic taste for your Butter Chicken is simpler with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key**: Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Smoke It Up (Optional)**: For an authentic Tandoori flavor, you can briefly smoke your cooked chicken. Place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small metal bowl inside the pan with the chicken, drizzle a few drops of oil or ghee over it, and quickly cover the pan for a few minutes. This infuses a wonderful smoky aroma.
*   **Smooth Sauce is a Must**: Blend the sauce thoroughly until absolutely no lumps remain. Straining it will give you that velvety restaurant-style texture.
*   **Quality Ingredients**: Use good quality canned tomatoes (San Marzano if possible) and fresh, full-fat dairy for the best flavor and richness.
*   **Kasoori Methi**: This dried fenugreek leaf is non-negotiable for authentic Murgh Makhani flavor. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma.
*   **Balance Sweetness**: The sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes and enhances the overall flavor. Adjust to your preference.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is incredibly versatile and pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice dishes. For a truly satisfying Indian lunch, consider these pairings:

*   **Naan Bread**: Soft, fluffy naan is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delightful.
*   **Roti or Chapati**: For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or chapati make an excellent accompaniment.
*   **Basmati Rice**: Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast to the creamy curry and helps soak up the flavors.
*   **Side Salad**: A simple cucumber and onion salad or a refreshing raita (yogurt with cucumber and mint) can cut through the richness and provide a cooling element.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment with variations to suit your dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani**: Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas for a delicious vegetarian alternative. Marinate paneer or tofu similarly to the chicken.
*   **Dairy-Free Option**: For a dairy-free version, use coconut cream or a cashew-based cream instead of heavy cream, and use a plant-based butter alternative.
*   **Spice Level**: If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce.
*   **Grilling the Chicken**: Instead of pan-frying, you can grill the marinated chicken in an oven, air fryer, or on an outdoor grill for a more authentic tandoori flavor.
*   **Add Vegetables**: While not traditional, you could add some blanched peas or bell peppers towards the end of the cooking process for added texture and nutrition.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a meal; it's a culinary experience that brings warmth and comfort. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a novice in the kitchen, this engaging Indian lunch recipe is surprisingly straightforward to master and promises to delight your taste buds with its harmonious blend of flavors. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be transported to the bustling streets of Delhi with every creamy, flavorful bite.
